features.loansText: Notes Receivable from Participants Participants may borrow from their fund accounts a minimum of $1,000 up to a maximum equal to the lessor of $50,000 or 50% of their vested account balance.

features.vestingText: Vesting – Participants are immediately vested in their voluntary contributions and any “safe harbor” profit sharing contributions plus actual earnings thereon.

features.matchText: The Company may make discretionary employer matching contributions.
features.vestingText: For any discretionary company non-elective contributions and earnings thereon, participants are fully vested after three years of service.
features.rothText: Participant accounts: Each participants’ account is credited with participant deferrals, Roth deferrals, company discretionary non-elective contributions and plan earnings.
features.loansText: (CONTINUED) Notes receivable from participants: Participants may borrow from their fund accounts a minimum of $1,000 up to a maximum equal to the lesser of 50% of their vested account balances or $50,000.

features.rothText: Accounts with a balance less than $5,000 (excluding Roth contributions) were rolled over to an individual retirement plan designated by the administrator, and a balance of less than $1,000 was distributed to the participant by the administrator without the participant’s consent.

features.matchText: For the year ended , the Company made discretionary matching contributions of 50 cents for every one dollar contributed by each participant, up to a maximum of three percent of participant’s eligible compensation.

features.rothText: In addition, participants may contribute designated Roth (after tax) contributions.
features.eligibilityText: Eligible employees automatically enter the Plan immediately following satisfaction of the eligibility requirements.
features.loansText: Notes Receivable from Participants Participants may borrow from their accounts a minimum of $1,000 up to a maximum amount equal to the lesser of: a) $50,000 reduced by the highest outstanding note receivable balance over the prior twelve months, or b) 50% of their vested account balance.
features.autoEnrollText: Automatically enrolled participants have their deferral rate set at 3% of eligible compensation and their contributions invested in a designated balanced fund until changed by the participant.

features.matchText: The Plan also allows safe harbor matching contributions equal to 100 percent of employee contributions up to 3 percent of participant compensation and 50 percent of employee contributions of the next 2 percent of participant compensation.
features.vestingText: Vesting Participants are immediately 100 percent vested in employee salary and rollover contributions and safe harbor matching contributions and the allocated earnings thereon.
features.loansText: Participant Notes Receivable Participants may borrow from their accounts a minimum of $1,000 up to a maximum equal to the lesser of $50,000 or 50 percent of their account balances.

features.vestingText: During 2024 and 2023, Company contributions were reduced by $0 and $97,720, respectively, from forfeited non-vested accounts.
features.loansText: Notes Receivable from Participants Participants may borrow from their fund accounts a minimum of $1,000 up to a maximum equal to the lesser of $50,000 or 50 percent of their vested account balance.
features.autoEnrollText: Additionally, the Plan was amended to provide for automatic enrollment of employees hired on or after November 1, 2016 and a one-time automatic enrollment for current active employees not participating or participating at a deferral percentage of less than 3% of their compensation.

features.matchText: For participants with 20 or more years of service, an additional 10% match is made by the Company on the first 6% of eligible compensation.
features.vestingText: A participant vests 20% per year and is 100% vested after five years of credited service.
features.rothText: The Plan was amended on April 15, 2019 to permit participants to contribute Roth after-tax deferrals, rollovers and in-plan conversions.
features.loansText: Notes Receivable from Participants Participants may borrow from their accounts a minimum of $1,000 up to a maximum amount equal to the lesser of $50,000 or 50% of their account balance.
features.autoEnrollText: If an employee does not submit an automatic enrollment waiver, he or she will be automatically enrolled in the Plan at a 6% deferral rate.

features.vestingText: A participant was 20% vested after one year of credited service and vested an additional 20% per year thereafter up to 100% vested after five years of credited service.

features.matchText: Employer Contributions The Company’s employer matching contribution is discretionary and determined at year-end.
features.vestingText: A participant is fully vested after four years of credited service or in the event a participant becomes disabled or dies.
features.loansText: Notes Receivable from Participants Participants may borrow the lesser of $50,000 or 50% of their vested account balance, subject to a $1,000 minimum and certain other restrictions.

features.rothText: In addition, Roth 401(k) contributions (after-tax) were permitted.
features.afterTaxText: In no event could participant pre-tax and after-tax contributions exceed any statutory limitations.

features.matchText: For 2023, the Company elected to make a discretionary matching contribution equal to one-half of the first 8% of base compensation that a participant contributed to the Plan each pay period.
features.vestingText: A participant was 100% vested after four years of credited service.
features.rothText: Participants had the option of contributing a percentage of their taxable compensation as a Roth contribution.
features.eligibilityText: All full-time employees of the Company, other than those covered by a collective bargaining agreement, were eligible to participate in the Plan after attaining the age of 21 and could enter the Plan on the first of the month following 30 days of employment.
